summary: Closed, unmerged path-slice PR for MN/menu matching improvements split out of a larger branch (#2617). The supplied diff slice only shows small `src/melee/mn/mnmain.c` changes: one call argument changed in `fn_8022AFEC` and local declaration ordering/hoisting in `mn_8022B3A0` to improve matching. Review feedback flagged a separate MN name-entry hunk as a regression when raw archive symbol strings were replaced by variables, reinforcing that literal asset-name strings should be preserved when they are needed for matching/data identity.
what_changed: In `src/melee/mn/mnmain.c`, `fn_8022AFEC` changed the third argument to `mn_8022A440` from `mn_804A04F0.prev_menu` to `data->hovered_selection` in the shown hunk.

In `mn_8022B3A0`, local declarations were reordered: `StaticModelDesc* top = &MenMainConTop_Top;` was moved earlier near the stack arrays and `HSD_GObj* gobj;` was moved with it, while the later `top` declaration was removed.
The PR body describes this as an isolated `src/melee/mn` split from a broader matching-improvement branch, intended to make review easier by directory/subsystem.

Although the PR body listed several MN files, the provided changed-files metadata for this slice reports only `src/melee/mn/mnmain.c` with 3 added and 3 deleted lines. Review comments reference `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c`, so that feedback may come from a broader or earlier slice context rather than the final provided diff excerpt.
smart_moves: The PR attempted to reduce review burden by splitting a larger matching-improvement branch by subsystem/pathspec, here focused on `src/melee/mn`.

The `mn_8022B3A0` declaration reordering is a typical low-risk decomp tactic: adjust local variable lifetimes/order without changing runtime behavior to influence register allocation and stack layout.
The PR included local hygiene checks in its body, notably applying the slice from fresh `origin/master` and passing `git diff --check`.
decomp_lessons: For menu/MN matching work, small source-level expression choices can matter: passing a struct field such as `data->hovered_selection` instead of a global field like `mn_804A04F0.prev_menu` may be required to match the original codegen, but the semantic correctness should be reviewed carefully.

Declaration order and initialization placement can affect matching through register allocation and stack slot placement; moving initialized locals such as `StaticModelDesc* top` earlier can be a valid matching tactic when evidence supports it.
Do not replace literal archive/resource strings with named variables if doing so changes generated code or data references; reviewers explicitly called this a regression in the MN name-entry context.

Path-sliced PRs should keep changed-file metadata and review context aligned. Here, the body/review mention files beyond the reported final changed file, which weakens confidence when reconstructing exact landed intent.
naming_conventions: Existing MN globals/functions retain project-style names such as `mn_804A04F0`, `mn_8022A440`, `mn_80229A04_dontinline`, `fn_8022AFEC`, and `mn_8022B3A0`.
Menu asset/model descriptors use names like `MenMainConTop_Top` and `MenMainBack_Top`, with archive string literals such as `"MenMainBack_Top_joint"` preserved in reviewer feedback.
Temporary and inferred locals in unmatched/decomp work use descriptive-but-provisional names such as `hovered_selection`, `selection_changed`, `cursor_jobj`, `root_jobj`, `top`, and `gobj`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reorder local declarations to influence register assignment and stack layout, as seen in `mn_8022B3A0`.
Move initialized pointer locals closer to the top of a function when that better matches compiler scheduling/lifetime behavior.
Select the exact source expression for an argument (`data->hovered_selection` versus `mn_804A04F0.prev_menu`) because equivalent-looking values may produce different loads/registers.
Preserve literal strings for archive/resource lookups when replacing them with variables is known to regress matching.
review_feedback: PsiLupan commented on `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c` that replacing a string with a variable is a regression, noting this had been mentioned on other PRs.

The cited review hunk showed literals like `"MenMainBack_Top_joint"`, `"MenMainBack_Top_animjoint"`, `"MenMainBack_Top_matanim_joint"`, and `"MenMainBack_Top_shapeanim_joint"` being replaced by variables such as `mnNameNew_803EE38C`; this was rejected as regressive.
No approving review or merge is present in the supplied context; the PR was closed with `merged_at: null`.
follow_up_queries: Look up PR #2617 for the full unsplit MN/matching context and whether these exact `mnmain.c` changes landed elsewhere.
Search later PRs touching `src/melee/mn/mnmain.c` around `fn_8022AFEC` and `mn_8022B3A0` to see the accepted final matching form.
Search for reviewer phrase `Replacing the string with the variable is a regression` to identify the broader convention about preserving archive/resource string literals.
Inspect whether `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c` was actually changed in an earlier revision of this PR despite the final changed-files metadata only listing `mnmain.c`.
